## Highlights

`feishu-md-sync@0.2.0` expands safe scoped publishing across the document elements used most often in technical documentation.

- Publish reconstructable HTML tables as scoped Feishu table updates, including one-level lists inside cells and row-level planning.
- Convert standalone PNG references with same-name local SVG files into editable Feishu Whiteboards with `--sync-whiteboards`.
- Publish note and warning Callouts while preserving Feishu-managed titles, emoji, colors, container identity, and unrelated remote child edits.
- Publish top-level fenced Code blocks with language aliases, caption preservation, creation, update, deletion, movement, and section reconcile.
- Inspect scope-aware status, diff, conflict, safety-gate, and semantic receipt information for tables, Callouts, Code blocks, and Whiteboards.

## Installation

```bash
npm install --global feishu-md-sync@0.2.0
feishu-md-sync --help
```

The official `lark-cli` continues to provide Feishu authentication and document IO. `feishu-md-sync` provides the local Markdown synchronization workflow, receipts, safety gates, planning, conflict detection, and verified writes.

## Safety model

Scoped publishing fails closed when correspondence is ambiguous, local and remote edits overlap, or readback verification differs from the planned result. Whole-document replacement is never used as an automatic fallback.

See the [documentation site](https://liyun95.github.io/feishu-md-sync/) for Quickstart, supported Markdown structures, configuration, and troubleshooting.
